economic_notes: Exclusive fish sales agreement with Premium Seafoods Limited expiring December 2023. Operations include fisheries, trout farm, One Stop convenience store, contracting company, gaming, and housing. Significant fisheries revenue of $16.1M in 2020 vs $9M in 2019.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2020. Three consolidated subsidiaries: We'koqma'q One Stop, We'koqma'q Housing Projects, and We'koqma'q Contracting Incorporated. Net debt position of $18.9M. HST payable dispute with CRA dating back to 2004-2005 still being repaid at $8,000/month. Bank indebtedness of $1.93M (line of credit). Current liabilities calculated as bank indebtedness + payables + HST payable = $1,932,446 + $4,171,509 + $1,237,617. Expenses_housing combines subsidized housing ($721,865) and non-subsidized housing ($2,573,909). Segmented salaries total ($13,048,541) differs significantly from consolidated salaries line ($2,558,709) — the consolidated statement appears to present salaries net or partially within other expense lines like Purchases and Repairs; the Schedule A total is used for expenses_salaries_total. No remuneration schedule included in the document.
